steps.title: Substitute into the function
steps.content: The function is $f(x)=7+\sqrt{x}$, so $f(169)$ means replace $x$ with $169$: $$f(169)=7+\sqrt{169}.$$

steps.title: Evaluate the square root
steps.content: The principal square root of $169$ is $13$. Therefore, $$f(169)=7+13=20.$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: B is correct. The common trap is to mishandle the square root or evaluate the function at a different input; this question only asks for the value when $x=169$.
